Tests the behavior of the delete operator on a non-existent variable.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that tests the behavior of the `delete` operator in JavaScript. It asserts that attempting to delete a non-existent variable, `i_dont_exist`, returns `true`. This demonstrates that the `delete` operator in JavaScript does not throw an error when used on a non-existent variable and instead returns `true`, indicating the operation was successful. The script provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on the behavior of the `delete` operator with undefined variables.
